# Job 11

## Verses (20)

| # | Basic English Bible | Compare |
|---|---|---|
| [1](BBE.md#v1) | Then Zophar the Naamathite made answer and said, | [WEB](WEB.md#v1) · [KJV](KJV.md#v1) · [ASV](ASV.md#v1) |
| [2](BBE.md#v2) | Are all these words to go unanswered? and is a man seen to be right because he is full of talk? | [WEB](WEB.md#v2) · [KJV](KJV.md#v2) · [ASV](ASV.md#v2) |
| [3](BBE.md#v3) | Are your words of pride to make men keep quiet? and are you to make sport, with no one to put you to shame? | [WEB](WEB.md#v3) · [KJV](KJV.md#v3) · [ASV](ASV.md#v3) |
| [4](BBE.md#v4) | You may say, My way is clean, and I am free from sin in your eyes. | [WEB](WEB.md#v4) · [KJV](KJV.md#v4) · [ASV](ASV.md#v4) |
| [5](BBE.md#v5) | But if only God would take up the word, opening his lips in argument with you; | [WEB](WEB.md#v5) · [KJV](KJV.md#v5) · [ASV](ASV.md#v5) |
| [6](BBE.md#v6) | And would make clear to you the secrets of wisdom, and the wonders of his purpose! | [WEB](WEB.md#v6) · [KJV](KJV.md#v6) · [ASV](ASV.md#v6) |
| [7](BBE.md#v7) | Are you able to take God's measure, to make discovery of the limits of the Ruler of all? | [WEB](WEB.md#v7) · [KJV](KJV.md#v7) · [ASV](ASV.md#v7) |
| [8](BBE.md#v8) | They are higher than heaven; what is there for you to do? deeper than the underworld, and outside your knowledge; | [WEB](WEB.md#v8) · [KJV](KJV.md#v8) · [ASV](ASV.md#v8) |
| [9](BBE.md#v9) | Longer in measure than the earth, and wider than the sea. | [WEB](WEB.md#v9) · [KJV](KJV.md#v9) · [ASV](ASV.md#v9) |
| [10](BBE.md#v10) | If he goes on his way, shutting a man up and putting him to death, who may make him go back from his purpose? | [WEB](WEB.md#v10) · [KJV](KJV.md#v10) · [ASV](ASV.md#v10) |
| [11](BBE.md#v11) | For in his eyes men are as nothing; he sees evil and takes note of it. | [WEB](WEB.md#v11) · [KJV](KJV.md#v11) · [ASV](ASV.md#v11) |
| [12](BBE.md#v12) | And so a hollow-minded man will get wisdom, when a young ass of the field gets teaching. | [WEB](WEB.md#v12) · [KJV](KJV.md#v12) · [ASV](ASV.md#v12) |
| [13](BBE.md#v13) | But if you put your heart right, stretching out your hands to him; | [WEB](WEB.md#v13) · [KJV](KJV.md#v13) · [ASV](ASV.md#v13) |
| [14](BBE.md#v14) | If you put far away the evil of your hands, and let no wrongdoing have a place in your tent; | [WEB](WEB.md#v14) · [KJV](KJV.md#v14) · [ASV](ASV.md#v14) |
| [15](BBE.md#v15) | Then truly your face will be lifted up, with no mark of sin, and you will be fixed in your place without fear: | [WEB](WEB.md#v15) · [KJV](KJV.md#v15) · [ASV](ASV.md#v15) |
| [16](BBE.md#v16) | For your sorrow will go from your memory, like waters flowing away: | [WEB](WEB.md#v16) · [KJV](KJV.md#v16) · [ASV](ASV.md#v16) |
| [17](BBE.md#v17) | And your life will be brighter than day; though it is dark, it will become like the morning. | [WEB](WEB.md#v17) · [KJV](KJV.md#v17) · [ASV](ASV.md#v17) |
| [18](BBE.md#v18) | And you will be safe because there is hope; after looking round, you will take your rest in quiet; | [WEB](WEB.md#v18) · [KJV](KJV.md#v18) · [ASV](ASV.md#v18) |
| [19](BBE.md#v19) | Sleeping with no fear of danger; and men will be desiring to have grace in your eyes; | [WEB](WEB.md#v19) · [KJV](KJV.md#v19) · [ASV](ASV.md#v19) |
| [20](BBE.md#v20) | But the eyes of the evil-doers will be wasting away; their way of flight is gone, and their only hope is the taking of their last breath. | [WEB](WEB.md#v20) · [KJV](KJV.md#v20) · [ASV](ASV.md#v20) |
